name: Roast Mallard with Root Vegetables- Country
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Small parsnips, peeled and trimmed, or medium-size parsnips, cut into 3 by 1/2-inch sticks
2 Bosc pears, peeled, halved, and cored
1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart saucepan, heat water to boiling. Add carrots and parsnips; cover and return water to boiling. Simmer root vegetables over low heat for 15 minutes to partially cook. Remove with slotted spoon to a small baking pan (set aside pan of cooking water). Add oil, salt, and pepper to vegetables; toss and set aside.
2. Remove necks, giblets, and discard any fat from duck cavities. Rinse ducks, necks, and giblets; pat dry. To reserved pan of cooking water, add necks and giblets of ducks and remaining salt and pepper. Heat to boiling; simmer covered over low heat for 45 minutes.
3. Preheat oven to 400°F. Insert apple quarters into neck and body cavities of each duck; twist wings back and under ducks to hold neck skin in place. Tie legs of each duck together. With fork, pierce breast skin. Arrange ducks, breast sides down, on wire rack in large roasting pan. Roast ducks with pan of root vegetables for 15 minutes. Turn ducks and continue to roast for 15 to 25 minutes longer or until breast meat is set but still red and moist when cut at the center of the thickest part.
4. Meanwhile, in a 2-quart saucepan, heat cranberry juice, wine, sugar, and cloves to boiling. Add pears; cover and return to boiling. Simmer pears over low heat for 7 to 10 minutes or until fork tender. Transfer pears to plate. Strain and reserve the pear poaching liquid.
5. Drain juices from body cavities of ducks into roasting pan. Discard apple quarters. Transfer ducks and root vegetables to platter; cover and keep warm. Pour giblet broth into roasting pan; heat to boiling, stirring to loosen browned bits. Strain broth mixture into a large measuring cup; discard fat on top and pour reserved pear poaching liquid into skillet. Add onion and cook over high heat for 1 minute. Add reserved pear poaching liquid. Boil rapidly until mixture is reduced to about two-thirds cup or syrupy consistency. Turn heat to low and add butter, stirring until it blends in. Pour sauce into a pitcher. Serve with pears.
